vocab.meaning: sự chủ động/tự quyền trên lộ trình
vocab.example: I lean towards being the driver because I relish having complete autonomy on the road, choosing my own pace and shortcuts.
As a passenger, I can simply recline the seat and drift off to sleep, which is a luxury a driver can never afford.
Navigating through heavy city traffic can be quite nerve-wracking, so I often prefer to let someone else take the wheel.
There is a certain thrill in being able to command the vehicle and feel the immediate response of the engine.

vocab.meaning: góc nhìn từ phía hành khách
vocab.example: I enjoy the passenger-side perspective as it allows me to appreciate the architecture and scenery that a driver might miss.
On long-haul journeys, I prefer to delegate the responsibility of driving to my brother so I can focus on reading.
Drivers often feel a visceral connection with the car, sensing every vibration and adjustment in the steering.

vocab.meaning: được chở đi (bởi tài xế riêng)
vocab.example: If I had the choice, I’d love to be chauffeured around all day so I could maximize my productivity on my laptop.
Driving requires a high cognitive load to monitor surroundings, whereas being a passenger is mentally effortless.

vocab.meaning: vị trí quan sát thuận lợi
vocab.example: Sitting in the back provides a different vantage point, making it easier to engage in conversation with everyone in the car.
